Football.title: ‘We don’t need to see that!’ – Sky Sports presenter forced to issue swift apology after fan’s X-rated background stunt
Football.content:
SKY SPORTS presenter Vicky Gomersall was forced to apologise after a man mooned the camera in the background of a live interview.

Gomersall and Sky Sports News co-host Jay Bothroyd were talking to Sunderland fan Taff in the wake of the Black Cats’ 1-1 draw with Bournemouth on Saturday.



But both the interviewers and the interviewee were caught off guard when a man dressed in dark clothing appeared behind Taff’s shoulder facing away from the camera before pulling his trousers down.



Gomersall immediately stepped in to take control of the situation, apologising profusely and bringing the interview to a close.

When the prankster appeared on camera, she intervened to say: “Do we want to come off that screen a bit, we don’t need to be seeing something like that, I can only apologise.”

She then wrapped things up, adding: “Taff I’m going to leave you there because we just don’t wanna have things like that, and if I can apologise to anyone watching at home that might have been offended by that, I certainly was.

“Apologies for that, unfortunately we do get some idiots like that when we are doing a live show.”



While Gomersall was clearly mortified at the strange incident, fans on social media found it hilarious.

One fan commented on TikTok: “The game is so back.”

Another joked: “The cheek of him.”

SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN



While a third fan labelled it the: “Best thing that has happened on Sky sports for years.”

While his co-host attempted to control the situation, Bothroyd was just trying to control his reaction to the shocking moment, and appeared to be holding back laughter.

There was no doubt that the former Coventry City, Cardiff and Wolves forward cracked a wry smile as the pair processed the odd scene.

Fans quickly caught on, with one joking: “(Bothroyd) is really trying his best to hold it in.”

And another added: “He is deffo wanting to laugh his head off.”

Football.title: Michail Antonio: Clubs refused to look at me because of my accident, my injury… I had to swallow ego to seal transfer
Football.content:
PREMIER LEAGUE clubs and owners rejected Michail Antonio after a horror car accident left him with a broken leg.

That is according to the former West Ham striker, who has finally found a new team more than six months after starting to search.





Antonio, 35, is preparing to make his long-awaited return after signing for Qatari outfit Al-Sailiya on a two-month deal until the end of the season.

He has played three times for Jamaica in June — all off the bench — but found nobody willing to take a risk on his fitness.

The forward told the BBC: “I’ve still got the qualities I had in the Premier League for the past 10 years.



“It shows, because every single manager was putting a contract in front of me once I trained with them.

“But there were managers and clubs who refused to look at me because of what happened – the accident, the injury.

“Some owners went against it.

“In football, the manager can want you, but it’s the owner’s money.



“My agent kept calling clubs and the same thing happened — clubs wanted me to train first.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“With the ego I had, I said: ‘I’m not coming to train. You’ve seen me play with Jamaica, you’ve seen my past 10 years. I shouldn’t have to train to get a contract.’

“Clubs said: ‘If you don’t train, we won’t sign you’.



“After staying at West Ham, training with the under-21s and going away with Jamaica again, my agent said: ‘You’re going to have to train, prove your fitness.’

“I had to swallow my ego – that’s how I ended up at Brentford.

“I trained with them for two weeks.”

Antonio left West Ham after 10 years there after not being in Graham Potter’s plans for the new season. He did play for the club’s Under-21s in August as part of his return to action.

He is their all-time top goalscorer in the Prem with 68 having risen through the English football pyramid from Tooting and Mitcham to Reading, Colchester, Southampton, Nottingham Forest, Sheffield Wednesday, and the West Ham.



But his last senior appearance for the Irons was back on December 3, 2024, in a 3-1 loss at Leicester.

Antonio fractured his leg in four places after crashing his Ferrari during Storm Darragh in Epping Forest.

The star, who does not remember the crash, admitted: “The hardest part wasn’t the recovery. It was the knockbacks for me.”

As he learned to move again properly, going through stages unable to walk before running and jumping, his attention turned to finding a new club.

But talks broke down after further injury issues cropped up.



Antonio explained: “When I found out I’d torn my calf the day before signing for Brentford, I laid in bed for two days.

“First day, I was just crying. The second day, I just didn’t want to get out of bed.

“I thought: ‘I’m back where I want to be, I’m back in the Premier League.’



“And then it happened again with Leicester.

“I was meant to return to Leicester but they didn’t want me back in because they didn’t want a relapse on their books.

“So I trained alone for a week and then went into Charlton.”

A pay-as-you-play deal with the Addicks was close, but Antonio opted for a switch to the Middle East.

He was recommended to Al-Sailiya by Jamaica team-mate and former Everton man Mason Holgate.



Holgate recommended Antonio after being asked for a potential free agent forward.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Antonio is now hoping to help his country qualify for the World Cup in the play-offs later this month.

He added: “Whatever happens in the summer, happens. I need to focus on the first goal — playing games and being fit.”

Football.title: Arsenal vs Chelsea preview: Betting tips, odds and free Premier League predictions
Football.content:
Commercial content notice: Taking one of the betting offers featured in this article may result in a payment to The Sun. 18+. T&Cs apply. gambleaware.org.

A blockbuster clash awaits as London rivals Arsenal and Chelsea meet in the Premier League at the Emirates on Sunday afternoon.

SunSport betting experts have previewed the contest and selected the best bets, tips and exclusive sign-up bonuses from our leading betting partners.



Find The Sun’s betting publishing principles here

Arsenal vs Chelsea preview

Not another one! Yes, Arsenal and Chelsea meet again for the fourth time in 91 days – this time back in the Premier League with a huge amount at stake.



Leaders Arsenal enter the weekend with a five-point advantage over title rivals Manchester City, who boast a crucial game in hand.

Many thought that cushion would evaporate last weekend, but ‘Dr Tottenham’ lent a helping hand to restore Arsenal’s mojo following their disappointing 2-2 draw with Wolves at Molineux.

The Gunners fired four past their London rivals that afternoon and will fancy their chances of collecting maximum points against another local opponent this weekend.

Arsenal have won their last three home league games against Chelsea, while the Blues are winless in their last eight Premier League meetings with the Gunners – their longest such run since a stretch of 19 between 1995 and 2005.

In his second game in charge of Arsenal in December 2019, Mikel Arteta lost 2-1 at the Emirates to Chelsea. Since then, his side have lost just one of 15 matches against the Blues in all competitions (W10 D4), beating them twice in the Carabao Cup semi-final earlier this year.

As for the visitors, their fortunes have been mixed of late.

Following a run of five wins on the spin, they’ve drawn their last two top-flight outings against Leeds and, inexplicably, Burnley – the latter conceding in stoppage time last week.

Those results have seen them slip out of the top four, with Liverpool now breathing down their necks. A return to winning ways is therefore paramount, but hopes aren’t high given their recent record against Arsenal and the fact they’ve lost eight of their last nine away Premier League matches against sides starting the day top of the table.

Football.title: Pep Guardiola's Man City situation 'feels like Sir Alex Ferguson' call as major hint given
Football.content:
Pep Guardiola's Man City situation 'feels like Sir Alex Ferguson' call as major hint given

Manchester City are looking to chase down Arsenal in the Premier League title race - and another triumph could pose questions about Pep Guardiola's next move at the Etihad

View 2 Images (Image: Getty Images)

Pep Guardiola has been tipped to go out in a blaze of glory if he wins the Premier League - much like Sir Alex Ferguson did in his Manchester United swansong.

‌



Manchester City closed to within two points of Arsenal after winning at Leeds on Saturday night. They continue to put pressure on leaders Arsenal, who can re-establish their five point lead, with the north Londoners having played a game more than their rivals.

‌



City have a history of chasing down Mikel Arteta's side in recent years and are looking to do so for a third time in four seasons. Guardiola and his players have claimed several titles and that experience is seen as a real plus.

‌



Arsenal meanwhile are trying to get over the line and, whilst they have a stellar playing squad, few of their players have won the Premier League previously.

Former top flight goalkeeper Shay Given can see Guardiola calling it quits at the Etihad if he claims another title, seeing comparisons with how Ferguson called time on his career after the 2013 Premier League triumph.

Man City shrug off Erling Haaland blow to cut Arsenal gap in Leeds win - 5 talking points READ MORE :

Pep Guardiola slams fans who booed Ramadan stoppage as Man City clash with Leeds paused READ MORE :

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

‌



He told Premier League Productions: "They [Arsenal] probably have the stronger squad because they have two or three players for every position, it seems. But Pep was interesting at his press conference [on Friday], what does Arteta need to do to get his hands on the trophy, he said, ‘I’m not going to tell him what to do’, because he’s got his eyes on the exact same trophy.

"It’s amazing, whatever team wins their remaining games wins the Premier League, it’s as simple as that. City are very much in the race and I would edge towards City.

View 2 Images Sir Alex Ferguson left in the aftermath of Man Utd's 2013 title (Image: Matthew Peters, Manchester United via Getty Images)

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

"I just feel they’ve been there before and Guardiola feels he wants to get his hands on it, a bit like when Fergie left [Manchester United], I’ve got a feeling he might leave if he does win the Premier League this season because he’s rebuilt his team now and it’s an opportunity for someone else to come in after him, but he wants to go out on a high no doubt."

Guardiola and City have continued to overhaul their squad over the past 18 months, setting themselves up for life post the Catalan. Marc Guehi, Tijjani Reijnders, Gianluigi Donnarumma and Antoine Semenyo have recently been signed. The likes of Erling Haaland and Ruben Dias are also in, of approaching, the peak of their careers.

Football.title: Cody Gakpo believes Liverpool are on cusp of 'something beautiful' after West Ham rout
Football.content:
Cody Gakpo believes Liverpool are on cusp of 'something beautiful' after West Ham rout

Arne Slot claimed his Liverpool team have played better than they did in the thrashing of West Ham but still lost and his players insist they are gearing up for a big end to the season

View 4 Images Cody Gakpo scored and Jeremie Frimpong forced an own goal in Liverpool's emphatic win over West Ham (Image: Getty Images)

Considering his team has a favourite’s chance of reaching the quarter-finals of the Champions League, is going strongly in the FA Cup and has some momentum in the Premier League, Arne Slot still looks like a manager under a spot of pressure. And considering his team won the Premier League last season, that seems a bit harsh, but there is no doubt Slot needs a good final three months of the campaign.

‌



Cody Gakpo thinks it could be more than good. He said: “Step by step, we’re getting to be a better team. We had a difficult moment during the season, but hopefully these last few games are the start of something beautiful.”

‌



Including the FA Cup victory over Brighton, Liverpool have won their last four games, scoring ten and conceding only twice. But even when scoring a handful against a self-destructive West Ham, it has not been beautiful.

‌



Liverpool legend Graeme Souness trapped in Abu Dhabi after strikes from Iran READ MORE :

Liverpool injury update given as manager confirms surgery and possible timeline READ MORE :

In the dismantling of Nuno Espirito Santo’s side, there were two goals that owed a lot to deflections (Hugo Ekitike’s opener and Gakpo’s hit for number four), one own goal (from Axel Disasi for Liverpool’s fifth), and two conversions of corner-kicks (the second and third goals from Virgil van Dijk and Alexis Mac Allister). The scoreboard was beautiful, the performance was not.

But it WAS highly effective. The only surprise was that Liverpool conceded quite a few chances, a couple of which were taken, Tomas Soucek and Taty Castellanos both reducing the arrears to two at stages of the second half.

View 4 Images Arne Slot has seen his team win their last three Premier League matches (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

‌



MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Despite going three goals to the good before half-time, Anfield was not exactly rocking but late cameos from Jeremie Frimpong, who forced the own goal, and 17-year-old Rio Ngumoha certainly got a few to their feet. A buzz was back.

Liverpool’s next three games could be season-defining. They travel to Wolves for a Premier League match and then for an FA Cup fifth round tie and then go to Istanbul for the first leg of their Champions League tie against Galatasary.

‌



View 4 Images Alexis Mac Allister and Hugo Ekitike were amongst the Liverpool scorers in the 5-2 win over West Ham (Image: Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

And Mac Allister believes this is the crucial time when Slot’s team can click into another gear, saying: “The last four or five months is when teams show what they can do. That is what we want.

"We know how important it is to qualify for the Champions League for the club and us as a team. The goal is there and we are going to do everything to qualify and be closer to the teams at the top.”

‌



In that bid to be closer to the teams at the top, set-pieces are clearly going to be very important. And Liverpool appear to finally have that side of the game cracked. Seven of Liverpool’s last nine goals have come from set-pieces - five from corners, one from a direct free-kick and one from a throw-in.

View 4 Images Virgil van Dijk was on target for the third time in his last six Premier League games (Image: Liverpool FC/Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

Slot said: “It was very pleasing. Firstly, because that is the reason we won. Second of all, people said well played and in my opinion, we have been better when we lost and better when we conceded from set-pieces.

Article continues below

“The first half of the season, almost every set-piece we conceded went in. Now, we start scoring from set-pieces and things start looking brighter and better than when you don’t.” Indeed. You could even say that things might even start to look beautiful again.

Football.title: 'Cristiano Ronaldo's agent picked me for Man Utd boss – I still want Premier League job'
Football.content:
'Cristiano Ronaldo's agent picked me for Man Utd boss – I still want Premier League job'

Jorge Mendes previously had a particular person in mind for the Manchester United job, and said manager has now opened the door to a Premier League comeback

View 2 Images Cristiano Ronaldo's former agent Jorge Mendes spoke to Man Utd chiefs (Image: Anadolu, Getty Images)

Cristiano Ronaldo's former agent recommended Julen Lopetegui for the Manchester United job in 2021 - and the former Wolves manager is now eyeing a return to the Premier League.

‌



United are currently managed by Michael Carrick in a caretaker capacity. The former club captain has been impressive so far, winning five of his six matches in charge and remaining undefeated.

‌



Carrick is in position until the end of the season and is building a strong case for the permanent job. A managerial hunt is expected to be finalised come the summer, with the World Cup proving a factor for numerous potential contenders - including Lopetegui.

‌



The ex-Premier League manager is currently the head coach of the Qatar national team after spells with Real Madrid, Wolves and West Ham. Jorge Mendes, who represented United legend Ronaldo, previously backed Lopetegui for the position after Ole Gunnar Solskjaer was dismissed in 2021.

Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Mendes was advocating for the 59-year-old to succeed Solskjaer whilst he was in charge at Sevilla. Carrick stepped in for his first interim spell and was succeeded by caretaker Ralf Rangnick, before Erik ten Hag assumed the full-time role.

‌



Lopetegui may have passed on the chance to move to Manchester when Mendes was pushing for his appointment, but he is now eager to make a comeback in the Premier League. Speaking to World Soccer, the current national team manager has openly admitted his ambition is to return to England's top flight.

When quizzed on a potential return to England, Lopetegui said: "Why not? For sure, one of my aims in the future is to come back to the Premier League again. But now we have a big, big aim in front of us – to go to one World Cup. My dream."

View 2 Images Now the Spaniard wants a Premier League comeback (Image: James Gill - Danehouse, Getty Images)

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

These comments could well place Lopetegui in the frame for United's next head coach vacancy. A host of names are said to be on the club's radar, amongst them Gareth Southgate and Oliver Glasner - though it is Carrick who supporters are rallying behind.

The 44-year-old is equally enthusiastic about the prospect, telling BBC Sport it remains his ultimate ambition to secure the role on a permanent basis.

Article continues below

He said: "It's not a stock answer - for me, it's the ultimate role. I'm really enjoying it, I love what I'm doing. I'm fortunate. I feel privileged to be in the position I am, but it's not the fact that I believe I can do it and I'm here to do it."

Carrick will come up against fellow United target Glasner at Old Trafford on Sunday afternoon when United face Crystal Palace. The Red Devils are aiming to secure yet another victory and continue their push for Champions League qualification.

Football.title: 'Arsenal didn't want me enough – I signed for Chelsea instead and won everything'
Football.content:
'Arsenal didn't want me enough – I signed for Chelsea instead and won everything'

Chelsea signed one of the greatest midfielders of their history following claims Arsenal didn't pursue the player enough

View 2 Images N'Golo Kante was a man in demand after Leicester City's title-winning (Image: Darren Walsh/Chelsea FC via Getty Images)

Arsenal are the favourites to triumph over Chelsea as the two sides meet in the Premier League this afternoon. However, a decade ago, it was the Blues who were consistently getting the better of their north London opponents.

‌



The two Premier League giants both expressed interest in acquiring N'Golo Kante following his outstanding performances in Leicester City's remarkable Premier League title-winning 2015/16 campaign. Yet, a transfer to the Emirates Stadium never came to fruition and he instead joined Chelsea in a £30million deal.

‌



Approaching the conclusion of his debut season at Stamford Bridge - which delivered his second consecutive Premier League crown - Kante revealed he turned down a switch to Arsenal, then managed by Arsene Wenger, because the club didn't pursue him with sufficient determination.

‌



In 2017, he told Sky Sports: "With Arsenal there was an interest but not a big as other clubs. That is why I didn't go to Arsenal and went to Chelsea." His spell at Stamford Bridge spanned seven years as he featured 269 times for the club and contributed to six major trophies, including the Champions League crown in the 2020/21 season when defeating Manchester City.

Before the European final against Pep Guardiola's team, Kante reflected on his footballing journey and disclosed how watching former Chelsea stars as a youngster inspired him to leave his mark on the sport.

Arsenal and Man City face having fixtures moved as Premier League issue strong statement READ MORE :

"Growing up, coaches or team-mates might tell me that I resemble such-and-such a player," he shared with UEFA. "I was just someone who watched football; I would watch the games which interested me, without necessarily focusing on players in a similar position to mine.

‌



View 2 Images N'Golo Kante won the Champions League with Chelsea

"Whenever the international break came around, I would obviously watch France play. And getting to watch the likes of [Claude] Makélélé or Lassana Diarra, I drew inspiration from them at some point.

"But in the end, I'm not like them. I was lucky to be in touch with them and they're great players with their own career, and I have my own career and path at my side. I have had some chats with Claude since I came to Chelsea.

‌



MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

"He was here to give me advice on my play, and the impact I can have on the game. Being able to talk with him about that is beneficial for me because he knows the position."

Following his departure from Chelsea in 2023, he joined Al-Ittihad on a free transfer and spent two years there before making his European football comeback this year with Fenerbahce.

Article continues below

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

The Blues haven't clinched a league title since 2017 but they have the opportunity to disrupt Arsenal's title pursuit when they face the league frontrunners in north London this afternoon.

Football.title: ‘I skipped Man Utd training – if the boss saw me I knew I wouldn’t get my transfer'
Football.content:
‘I skipped Man Utd training – if the boss saw me I knew I wouldn’t get my transfer'

An ex-Manchester United star accused them of lying to him after opting to leave his boyhood club

View 2 Images Dean Henderson was 14 years old when he first signed for Manchester United (Image: Getty Images)

Dean Henderson felt he had no choice but to steer clear of Erik ten Hag so he'd get to leave Manchester United. The Crystal Palace shot-stopper joined United's academy in 2011 aged 14, ultimately departing for good 12 years later with his 2023 switch to the Eagles.

‌



Henderson, 28, experienced his breakthrough campaign at Old Trafford during the 2020/21 season, where he featured 26 times across all competitions under Ole Gunnar Solskjaer.

‌



Nevertheless, he managed only three outings the subsequent season as United cycled through three managers, with Solskjaer being dismissed then Michael Carrick receiving the caretaker position before Ralf Rangnick's interim appointment.

‌



Following his season-long loan move to Nottingham Forest for the 2022/23 campaign, Henderson revealed details about the distressing season he endured at United, accusing the club of broken promises.

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Speaking to talkSPORT, Henderson said: "To be quite honest with you, it's probably been the toughest 12 months of my career. It's been tough, it's been hard and I'm so happy I've come out the other end of it now.

‌



Spanish media in agreement as Marcus Rashford returns after Barcelona transfer 'confirmed' READ MORE :

"The conversation I had, coming out of the Euros squad [with England], was that you're coming back here [to United] to be the No 1 goalkeeper. I got Covid, then unfortunately no-one followed through with anything they told me."

He said the circumstances were "criminal" as he turned down loan opportunities in pursuit of establishing himself as the club's first-choice goalkeeper. He continued: "It's frustrating as well, because I turned so many good loans down last summer for that reason and they wouldn't let me go.

View 2 Images Dean Henderson has flourished since signing for Crystal Palace (Image: Sebastian Frej/Getty Images)

‌



"To sit there and waste 12 months is criminal, really, at my age. I was fuming." Before his switch to the City Ground, Henderson deliberately avoided speaking to or working under Ten Hag, who took charge as United manager in the summer of 2022, in a bid to engineer a departure from Manchester.

The England shot-stopper revealed: "No. I didn't want him to see me in training. Because I knew that he probably wanted to keep me. So I told the hierarchy that I need to play football, I don't want to be here and play second fiddle."

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Following his exit from United, he's secured an FA Cup triumph and been appointed Palace skipper after Marc Guehi's transfer to Manchester City in January. Despite the turbulence he encountered with his previous employers, he acknowledged upon departing it had been a "dream come true" to have worn the Red Devils' colours.

He told MUTV: "It's a dream come true to have pulled on the shirt and played at Old Trafford, in European competitions, in FA Cups, Carabaos and, most importantly, the Premier League.

"That was something I really wanted to do from a young age. It'll be sad to leave." Henderson is now set to lead his team in their away match at Old Trafford on Sunday, as Palace aim to secure consecutive league victories for the first time since December.

Article continues below

Football.title: Leeds boss Daniel Farke to appeal Man City red card with 'ref cam' defence
Football.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Leeds boss Daniel Farke to appeal Man City red card with 'ref cam' defence

Daniel Farke was shown a red card for remonstrating with the referee following Leeds' defeat by Manchester City at Elland Road but will appeal the dismissal

View Image Farke was given his marching orders by referee Peter Bankes (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

Leeds United boss Daniel Farke will appeal his red card following his side's home defeat to Manchester City - and ask the FA to review 'ref cam' footage in a bid to get it overturned.

‌



Farke was dismissed after the final whistle at Elland Road as he approached referee Peter Bankes. But Farke claims the card was brandished before he had even said a word to the officials.

‌



The German was left stunned by the decision - and can be seen asking Bankes what he had been dismissed for.

‌



Farke has spoken to club officials about what happened - and believes footage from the camera Bankes had clipped to his ear for the game, will prove he did nothing wrong.

Pep Guardiola slams fans who booed Ramadan stoppage as Man City clash with Leeds paused READ MORE :

Leeds vs Man City stopped as fans boo during Ramadan break in clash READ MORE :

BACK THE MIRROR! Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search

‌



It's understood Leeds will lodge an official appeal to the FA. And the club believe there is a strong chance video footage will prove Farke has no case to answer.

Farke is now facing a ban, but is still expected to be on the touchline for Tuesday's crucial home game with Sunderland, because his case will not be dealt with in time.

Leeds assistant boss Edmund Riemer defended Farke. He said: "I am disappointed because he's never a manager who's over aggressive or over reacting. I think it's a very harsh decision."

Article continues below

Football.title: Inside Rangers and Celtic managers' fight that saw both men banned – 'I snapped'
Football.content:
Inside Rangers and Celtic managers' fight that saw both men banned – 'I snapped'

An explosive Old Firm bust-up led to Rangers and Celtic icons being charged and hit with touchline bans

View 3 Images Ally McCoist and Neil Lennon needed to be separated at the end of an Old Firm derby (Image: Sky Sports)

Rangers and Celtic icons were both slapped with bans after almost coming to blows in one of the most heated Old Firm clashes of all time. The Glasgow grudge match is never one for the faint-hearted, but the Scottish Cup replay meeting in March 2011 was on another level.

‌



Celtic won the tie 1-0 but the result was a sideshow compared to what transpired during and after the match. Rangers had three men sent off - Steven Whittaker and Madjid Bougherra for two bookable offences during the game, while El-Hadji Diouf picked up a second yellow for dissent after the full-time whistle.

‌



There was then more drama as Celtic boss Neil Lennon and Rangers assistant manager Ally McCoist clashed on the touchline and had to be pulled apart as a mass brawl threatened to break out. The pair - both steeped in their club's history from their playing days - were punished with touchline bans after being charged with misconduct by the Scottish FA.

‌



Diouf had already wound up the Celtic bench following Whittaker's red card when he clashed with physio Tim Williamson. That prompted a stern reaction from Lennon, who told Open Goal last year: "El Hadji Diouf started it all. [Celtic's] Emilio Izaguirre got injured, Steven Whittaker got sent off.

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

"Diouf was coming over to get instructions, and our physio, Tim Williamson, was going on to treat Emilio. Diouf gives one into Tim, and I’m actually delighted he done that, because it let me off the leash.

‌



Celtic vs Rangers chaos as star avoids red card despite kicking rival in the head READ MORE :

"So I clocked it and went, 'F***ing leave my staff alone.' Then Coisty comes out shouting, 'Don't talk to my players like that.' That was the start of it."

Given that earlier exchange, it wasn't a surprise when there was no friendly handshake between the pair at full-time, with the image of Lennon and McCoist going head to head now seared into Old Firm folklore.

Lennon said: "Instead of doing the normal thing – shake hands and deal with it later – I f***ing snapped. Then you’ve got Parkes (Celtic assistant boss Garry Parker) trying to split us up and getting punched by the fourth official, which was really, really funny.

‌



View 3 Images Lennon confronts El Hadji Diouf (Image: Getty)

"But at the time, I wasn't giving an inch. I knew how it worked. I wasn’t letting anyone get the upper hand on us if I could avoid it."

Lennon received a four-match touchline ban, while McCoist was hit with a two-game suspension. McCoist took over as Rangers boss at the end of that season, following the legendary Walter Smith's retirement.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

The pair have long since buried the hatchet and even socialise together. Speaking in 2015, Lennon said: "It made great television and it made great storylines for a long, long time. In 20 years, they'll be showing footage of me and Ally squaring up to each other.

"We probably realised after that we had behaved like big kids really, but that's what the [Old Firm] games do to you. We're still very much in touch.

‌



View 3 Images Lennon bears no grudge towards McCoist (Image: Getty Images)

"Many times we have shared a beer and we were texting each other over the Christmas period. Whether we were rivals or not, there was a healthy respect. It's only people who have been in the job [who] can understand what it's like."

There are unlikely to be such scenes when Rangers, managed by German Danny Rohl and Celtic, led by the legendary Martin O'Neill, meet at Ibrox on Sunday. Both sides need the three points as they look to keep pace with Scottish Premiership leaders Hearts.

Article continues below

Football.title: Hull KR and Leeds' night in Las Vegas 'the best and worst of times'
Football.content:
One of Charles Dickens' most-quoted lines is 'It was the best of times, it was the worst of times'. That is the best way to sum up Hull KR vs Leeds Rhinos in Las Vegas.

Unlike Dickens' novel though, this was not the 'A Tale of Two Cities' it was the tale of one, Las Vegas, in which Leeds Rhinos made their Betfred Super League statement.

For Leeds, it was truly a night to remember, a night in which Brad Arthur's side were faced with delivering for an occasion and they did that and then some.

Ten tries went in as Hull KR were utterly rudderless when faced with Leeds' spine in full flow and their forwards laying down the groundwork.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player The Leeds players celebrate with their travelling fans in Las Vegas after beating Hull KR

Maika Sivo, who scored four of those 10, got his reward after having to wait a year to make his mark in Super League. After flying his family over in 2025, he suffered an ACL injury that postponed his Leeds debut, but he has now scored six tries in two games.

The Leeds side is one that has proven winners but a lot of inexperience in the big games. In Las Vegas, they showed they could do it.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Leeds' Maika Sivo ran in four tries in an outstanding performance against Hull KR in Las Vegas

While Sivo grabbed the headlines, Jake Connor also deserves the plaudits. He had the Robins on a string and, as is his character, he enjoyed running the show and putting KR on edge while he stayed calm at the helm.

That calmness helped rattle, mystify and trouble Hull KR in a way you do not often see.

Hull KR beat Brisbane. Leeds beat Hull KR. They got to deliver their statement that they can be Super League contenders.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sivo barges over for the opening try in Las Vegas

"We wanted to make sure that we did ourselves proud and our fans and did the competition justice," Arthur said.

"We got an opportunity to come over and represent the Super League and represent the Leeds Rhinos.

"We had thousands of fans travel over and we had seen the support, so we weren't anticipating it to end that way but I was anticipating the toughness, the physicality, the real hunger that our boys showed.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sivo makes a huge break downfield and shifts it out wide for Ryan Hall to cruise over

"It is just our way of showing our appreciation for being given a great opportunity. We had a ball.

"The thing I was really looking forward to seeing was the big occasion.

"It is the biggest occasion our guys have played. As a group, since I've had them, this is the first time we are having a big occasion like this.

"In the past, we may have tightened up in that sort of situation and got a bit anxious, but we didn't today, so hopefully that's a step in the right direction for us."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sivo goes over for his fourth try in Las Vegas

Throughout the week, Leeds experienced the big moments - Fremont Street, fan signings, UFC - but constantly reiterated they were here with a job to do despite the bright lights. For their hard work, I am sure they will leave Vegas after having the 'best of times' with their team-mates.

For Hull KR, it is a completely opposite story. They experienced the bright lights and seemingly got dazzled by them. They walked in on top of the world, they walked down Fremont Street as the kings of rugby league.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Brodie Croft goes over to extend Leeds Rhinos' lead over Hull KR in Las Vegas

Now, they leave Las Vegas still on zero points in Super League, bottom of the table and with questions to answer when they head to Huddersfield next week.

It is a reminder for the Robins after taking four titles that the low moments still happen. It is now about how they bounce back from a pretty big one.

Together, as a team, they have to sit and lick the wounds of one of their 'worst times'.

How they respond to that will decide how and when they experience the 'best of times' once again.

2026 Super League - key dates and what to look out for

Super League's 30th birthday: Thu Mar 26: Castleford Tigers vs Bradford Bulls , 8pm (Sky Sports)
